criteria.description: Native acceptance boundary defines compatible operation, payload, status, metrics, and failure behavior without requiring a compiled extension.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: native acceptance fixture and bridge tests pass under Composer mode.

criteria.description: PHP-level compatibility fixture can be reused by a future native implementation.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: fixture accepts an injected invoker rather than hard-coding PhpExtensionBridge internals.

criteria.description: Sidecar durable resume request and response contract is defined with versioned sidecar envelopes.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: request/response tests assert envelope type, run ID, action, status, and metadata.

criteria.description: Durable resume behavior is demonstrated without running a sidecar process.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: fake or injectable transport example runs locally.

criteria.description: Enterprise adapter split decision records first package candidate, ownership boundary, likely future file movement, and timing rationale.
Composer mode remains the stable baseline and validates without native extension or running sidecar.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: composer check passes and deployment readiness keeps native runtime optional.

criteria.description: Roadmap and enterprise docs represent Phase 5.1 as runtime continuation work while preserving optional-native strategy.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: README, outline, and enterprise docs align on Composer-first wording.

negative_invariants.description: No test, example, or default SDK path may require a compiled native extension.
No test, example, or default SDK path may require a running sidecar service.
This mission must not physically split Composer packages.
